name: American Apple Pie
recipeIngredient: 1 cup Chilled shortening (lard, margarine, or unsalted butter), cut into bits
2 1/2 pounds Sweet-tart, firm apples (peeled, cored, quartered, sliced to equal 8 cups)
recipeInstructions:
1. Sift together flour and salt in a bowl with a mixer fitted with a paddle attachment.
2. Add shortening and beat on low speed until the mixture reaches the consistency of fine cornmeal.
3. Whisk together milk and egg in a small bowl, then pour into the flour mixture.
4. Increase speed to medium and beat until dough forms a ball and pulls away from sides of the bowl.
5. Divide dough into three equal portions.
6. Flatten each portion into disks and wrap separately in plastic wrap. Refrigerate two portions for at least 20 minutes or up to 1 day. Cover and refrigerate the third portion for another purpose for up to 2 days, or freeze up to 1 month.
7. On a heavily floured surface, roll out one portion of dough into an 1/8-inch thick round about 2 inches larger in diameter than a 9- or 10-inch pie plate.
8. Drape the dough over the rolling pin and carefully transfer it to the pie plate.
9. Press gently. Trim the dough so it hangs about 1/2 inch over the edge. Fold the overhang under and press against the rim.
10. Brush the bottom and sides of the crust with melted butter. Set aside.
11. Toss together apples, sugar, and flour in a large bowl. Add vanilla and half-and-half. Mix well.
12. Spoon filling into the prepared crust.
13. Roll out the second dough portion into a round of about the same size and thickness.
14. Using a sharp knife, cut the dough into strips 1/2 to 3/4 inch wide for a lattice top.
15. Pick up two longest strips at the center of the round and cross over the center of the filled pie crust.
16. Place half of the strips about 3/4 inch apart across the pie. Fold back every other strip from the center and place cross strips close to the center of the pie.
17. Unfold folded strips. Repeat in this manner, working from the center of the pie to the rim until the entire pie is covered with a woven pattern.
18. Trim ends of strips even with the edge of the bottom crust. Lift ends of strips and brush edges of the bottom crust with water.
19. Press strips gently but firmly against the rim. Flute the edges.
20. Bake at 425°F for 10 minutes. Reduce temperature to 350°F and bake until the crust is golden and apples are tender when pierced with a thin knife blade, about 30 to 40 minutes. Let cool completely on a rack.
21. Invert the aluminum pie plate over the top and secure in place with rubber bands or string, or cover with foil. Pack in a picnic basket.
